ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Pɔ̀ Yesu ebu lɛ onu aki yɛ si, “Ki tɔ lize nite abla. Niteme si, kwiabitɛ Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa.” Liki si Yohanes adimɛ ɛ.
translated: When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to get well?” “Sir,” the invalid replied, “I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am trying to get in, someone else goes down ahead of me.” This happened on the Sabbath.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ eze ní banima kusi, gi azɛɛnya ɛ. Ika lɛ izedo ní yɛ su agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa nyɔŋ.
translated: The man who has no relatives in the town, he is a stranger. If he dies, the vultures will eat him in the open field.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si Aya aakɔ̀ banima eboe zimizi kakaa ni ebusioboe kakaa gi biado e, kukɔkyɛ ba.
translated: If you do not want to marry me, then tell me so that I can go to my father's house and pack my things and my children's things and leave.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: “Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ pɔ́ si: xe mletsi kude ɛki kiditɔ ní kesea aʋa ɛ, biatsi kɛ onu ní lifunu ne, lɛ xe mlɛki kude ní kiditɔ su ní kesea aʋa ɛ, biaki kɛ kude ní lifunu ne.
translated: I will tell you a story: There was a certain man who had two children, a boy and a girl, and there was a certain woman who also had two children, a boy and a girl.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Lɛ bɛsɛ ní ɔnɔha lɔ su xe badɔkpasi ba bemu iku ní katapokpaa mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ Yesu xunyɔ ɛ, eze ní katapokpaa mɛ koko; ko lɛ ba petee bɛsɛ ɛ. Kutapokpa dzedzeo fɛ kwipɔni yɛ.
translated: When the woman saw that the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it. Then the eyes of both of them were opened.
